history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after jumping directly from Spring Boot 2.3.1 to 3.3, causing Spring Batch API signature mismatches and type incompatibilities.
history.qwen_rationale: The compatibility matrix warns that jumping straight to SB 3.3 from SB ≤ 2.5 has high regression risk. Stepping through SB 2.7 first under JDK 11 allows the project to adapt to intermediate API changes, then bumping to JDK 17, and finally upgrading to SB 3.3 under JDK 17 reduces the shock of the javax→jakarta and Spring Framework 5→6 transitions.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed at upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 due to Spring Batch API signature changes and type mismatches after dependency updates, likely exacerbated by running an intermediate SB 2.7 upgrade before the 3.x jump.
history.qwen_rationale: Following the empirical template for SB 2.x -> 3.x migrations, I replace the explicit upgrade_build_to_java17 step with upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 (which internally handles the Java 17 build configuration and dependency bumps) and remove the intermediate SB 2.7 step to reduce cumulative regression risk. This aligns with the recommended pattern and isolates the framework transition to a single, well-tested meta-recipe step under JDK 17.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed during the direct Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade due to breaking API changes in Spring Batch (missing JobRepository argument) and Hibernate/JPA date type conversions in custom controllers.
history.qwen_rationale: Stepping the Spring Boot upgrade incrementally (2.3 -> 2.7 -> 3.0 -> 3.3) across JDK boundaries reduces regression risk by applying framework migrations gradually. This allows intermediate compilation validation and gives OpenRewrite's transitive recipes a chance to resolve dependency shifts and signature changes before the final JDK 21 transition.

history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 3.0 upgrade step failed compilation due to unpatched Spring Batch API changes (JobControl method signatures) and type mismatches (LocalDateTime vs Date) in custom controllers.
history.qwen_rationale: The previous chain attempted a gradual Spring Boot upgrade (2.7 -> 3.0) which exposed unhandled API incompatibilities in Spring Batch 5.x. Following the empirical guidance for SB 2.x -> 3.x migrations, I will replace the intermediate upgrade_build_to_java17 step with the comprehensive UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe under JDK 17. This meta-recipe composes all necessary 2.x->3.x transitions and includes broader migration fixes that should resolve the framework-level API shifts before proceeding to Java 21.

history.qwen_observation: Build fails at Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade due to Spring Batch 5.x API changes and type mismatches in custom code, likely caused by jumping directly from SB 2.3 to 3.3 without an intermediate 2.7 stabilization step.
history.qwen_rationale: Following the empirical best practice to walk Spring Boot up to the highest compatible 2.x version (2.7) under the source JDK before bumping Java and proceeding to 3.x. This incremental path reduces regression risk and allows the build to stabilize at each major framework boundary before applying Java 17/21 transforms.
